name: Odysight.ai Inc.
business: Odysight.ai develops and sells visual monitoring AI solutions that use small vision-based sensors and edge AI to monitor critical components in hard-to-reach and harsh environments for predictive maintenance (PdM) and condition-based monitoring (CBM). The solutions serve both civil and defense sectors, including aerospace, industrial, transportation, and energy markets. Customers include the Israeli Air Force, NASA, Israel Railways, and various defense contractors.
namedCustomers: a leading European provider of elevator monitoring solutions

exposures.sentence: Our core technologies include: Vision-Based Sensor Technology: This innovative approach uses micro sensors to monitor and analyze system health condition in real-time, designed for hard-to-reach and harsh environments.

Edge AI Integration: Developing specialized AI models that process high-resolution visual data directly at the edge, enabling the detection of subtle physical anomalies before they result in malfunction or failure.
We specialize in predictive maintenance and condition-based monitoring, utilizing advanced AI-driven sensor visualization solutions, using video analytics and algorithms.

Our customers include the Israeli Air Force, the Israeli Ministry of Defense, a global international defense contractor, NASA, and Israel Railways Ltd., as well as a leading European provider of elevator monitoring solutions.
We are generating commercial revenues in aerospace, and advancing Industry 4.0 and transportation initiatives, from proof-of-concept and demos into commercial deliveries.
Our solutions are already deployed in the industrial, automotive and aviation sectors.

Our vision-based sensing technology provides solutions across diverse PdM and CBM markets, and in harsh conditions as demonstrated in a variety of environments, including outer space during NASA's Robotic Refueling Mission, or RRM3.

Our vision-based sensor technology Initially used in medical devices, our video-based sensor solution (comprising image acquisition, data collection and storage and image processing, including PdM and CBM) have contributed significantly to visual aids for medical teams in an FDA-cleared minimally invasive surgical device.

catalysts.sentence: On February 11, 2025, our common stock began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol ODYS.
In February 2025, we closed a public offering, including the exercise of an over-allotment option granted to the underwriter in the public offering.

On February 28, 2024, we formed D. VIEW Ltd., a private company organized under the laws of the State of Israel and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Odysight.ai Inc., to act as a local agent for the defense market in Israel.
On January 9, 2025, we formed an additional wholly-owned subsidiary, Odysight.Ai Eu S.r.l., a private company organized under the laws of Italy.

Our lease for proximately 800 square meters of office, manufacturing and laboratory space in Omer, Israel is set to expire in May 2026, at which time we have leased an alternative location in Omer consisting of approximately 286 square meters of space.
On May 21, 2023, we were awarded a grant by the Israel Innovation Authority, or the IIA.

tamClaims.claim: Global predictive maintenance market $14 billion in 2025, growing to $82 billion by 2031 (34% CAGR).
tamClaims.sentence: Based on industry reports and our estimates, the global predictive maintenance market was approximately $14 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow to approximately $82 billion by 2031, at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 34%.

tamClaims.claim: Aerospace TAM currently more than $10 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In the aerospace market alone, which includes commercial aviation, military rotorcraft, fixed wing aircraft and UAVs, we believe that our Aerospace TAM is currently more than $10 billion.
